title: Implement Trie (Prefix Tree)
description: Implement a trie with insert, search, and startsWith methods. A trie is a tree-like data structure used to store strings where each node represents a character. All descendants of a node share a common prefix.

examples.input:
trie = Trie()
trie.insert("apple")
trie.search("apple")
trie.search("app")
trie.startsWith("app")
trie.insert("app")
trie.search("app")

hints: Each node can have at most 26 children (lowercase letters).
Use a boolean flag to mark the end of a word.
For insert, create nodes for each character if they don't exist.
For search, traverse each character; return false if any node is missing or end flag is false.
For startsWith, same as search but don't require the end flag.
timeComplexity: O(m) for all operations, where m is the length of the word
spaceComplexity: O(n * m) total where n is number of words and m is average word length

title: Design Add and Search Words Data Structure
description: Design a data structure that supports adding new words and finding if a string matches any previously added string. The search function can contain dots ('.') which can match any letter.

examples.input:
wordDict = WordDictionary()
wordDict.addWord("bad")
wordDict.addWord("dad")
wordDict.addWord("mad")
wordDict.search("pad")
wordDict.search("bad")
wordDict.search(".ad")
wordDict.search("b..")
examples.output: [null, null, null, false, true, true, true]

hints: Use a trie to store words.
For the dot character, try all 26 possible children recursively.
DFS backtracking handles wildcard matching efficiently.
timeComplexity: O(m) for addWord, O(26^m) worst case for search with dots where m is word length
spaceComplexity: O(n * m) for storing n words of average length m

title: Word Search II
description: Given an m x n board of characters and a list of words, find all words from the dictionary that can be found on the board. Each word must be constructed from letters of sequentially adjacent cells (horizontally or vertically). The same cell may not be used more than once in a word.
hints: Build a trie from all words in the dictionary.
Traverse the board using DFS from each cell.
Use the trie to prune branches that can't match any word.
Mark visited cells temporarily and restore them after DFS.
Remove found words from the trie to avoid duplicates.
timeComplexity: O(m * n * 4 * 3^(L-1)) where L is max word length, m*n is board size
spaceComplexity: O(N) where N is total number of characters in all words

title: Replace Words
description: In English, we have a concept called root form, from which some words were formed. Given a dictionary of many roots and a sentence, replace all the words in the sentence with the shortest root form from the dictionary. If a root has the same length as the word, don't replace it.

examples.input:
dictionary = ["cat","bat","rat"]
sentence = "the cattle was rattled by the battery"
examples.output: "the cat was rat by the bat"

hints: Insert all roots into a trie.
For each word in the sentence, find the shortest prefix in the trie.
If found, replace the word with the root.
timeComplexity: O(n + m + s) where n is total chars in roots, m is number of words, s is total chars in sentence

title: Map Sum Pairs
description: Design a map that allows you to do the following: Insert a key-value pair, or find the sum of the values of all keys that start with a given prefix. Each key is a string, and each value is an integer.

examples.input:
mapSum = MapSum()
mapSum.insert("apple", 3)
mapSum.sum("ap")
mapSum.insert("app", 2)
mapSum.sum("ap")

hints: Use a trie where each node stores the sum of all values in its subtree.
When inserting, update the sum at each node along the path.
For sum query, traverse to the prefix node and return its stored sum.
timeComplexity: O(m) for insert and sum where m is the key length
spaceComplexity: O(n * m) where n is number of keys and m is average key length

title: Stream of Characters
description: Design an algorithm that accepts a stream of characters and checks if a suffix of these characters is any string in a given list of words. Implement the StreamChecker class with a query method that returns true if any suffix matches a word in the dictionary.

examples.input:
streamChecker = StreamChecker(["cd","f","kl"])
streamChecker.query('a')
streamChecker.query('b')
streamChecker.query('c')
streamChecker.query('d')
streamChecker.query('e')
streamChecker.query('f')

hints: Reverse each word and insert into the trie so you can search suffixes forward.
Keep a buffer of recent characters up to the maximum word length.
For each query, search the trie using the reversed recent characters.
timeComplexity: O(q * L) per query where q is query count and L is max word length
spaceComplexity: O(n * L + L) where n is number of words and L is max word length

title: Search Suggestions System
description: Given an array of products and a search word, design a system that suggests at most three product names after each character of the search word is typed. Suggestions should have a common prefix with the search word and be sorted lexicographically.
hints: Sort the products array first.
Build a trie from sorted products.
For each prefix of the search word, collect up to 3 lexicographically smallest matches using DFS.
Use early termination when 3 results are collected.
timeComplexity: O(n * m * log(n) + q * m) where n is products count, m is word length, q is searchWord length

title: Maximum XOR of Two Numbers in an Array
description: Given an integer array nums, return the maximum result of nums[i] XOR nums[j] where 0 <= i <= j < n. Solve this using a binary trie.
hints: Convert each number to a 32-bit binary representation.
Insert each number into a binary trie bit by bit (from MSB to LSB).
For each number, greedily traverse the trie trying to go to the opposite bit to maximize XOR.
The maximum XOR is found by trying all numbers.
timeComplexity: O(n * 32) = O(n) where n is the array length
spaceComplexity: O(n * 32) = O(n) for the binary trie

title: Longest Word in Dictionary
description: Given an array of strings words representing an English dictionary, return the longest word in words that can be built one character at a time by other words in words. If there is more than one possible answer, return the longest word with the smallest lexicographical order. If there is no answer, return the empty string.
hints: Insert all words into a trie.
A word is valid if every prefix exists as a word (except the first character which is always valid).
Use DFS from the root, only traversing nodes marked as end-of-word.
Track the best word found during DFS.
timeComplexity: O(n * m) where n is number of words and m is average word length

title: Palindrome Pairs
description: Given a list of unique words, find all pairs of distinct indices (i, j) in the given list such that the concatenation of the two words, i.e., words[i] + words[j], is a palindrome.
hints: Reverse each word and insert into a trie. Store the index of each word.
For each word, traverse the trie. At each node, check if the remaining suffix is a palindrome.
If the remaining suffix is a palindrome and there's a word ending at that node, it's a valid pair.
Handle the case where the trie word is shorter (remaining part of original word must be palindrome).
timeComplexity: O(n * k^2) where n is number of words and k is average word length
